_meta.title: Duet / RepRapFirmware — neutral intent → GCode command map
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (RRF GCode dictionary); M568 A0/A1/A2 confirmed against official docs (shippable without hardware); M23/M24 auto-start semantics 🔵 community

derived_from.section: Writing / control — a curated intent → GCode map
derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from the RRF GCode dictionary. Each mapping is an uncopyrightable interface fact; verify against the dictionary.

notes: RRF is GCode-native — there are NO structured setpoint endpoints; every action is a GCode string. Emit curated, typed, safe GCode from a registry and gate arbitrary user GCode off by default.
⚠️ Use M568 for tool temps/state (RRF 3.3+), NOT `M104 T<n> S…` (that is the Marlin-lineage form). G10 stays valid for OFFSETS only.

Command channel differs by mode; the GCode vocabulary is identical. Standalone: GET /rr_gcode?gcode=<codes> (response {"bufferSpace":int}; reply text via GET /rr_reply). SBC: POST /machine/code (raw body).
Gate motion mid-print; gate tool-select / e-stop / firmware-reset / config-writes harder still.

intents.gcode: G91 ; G1 X.. Y.. Z.. F<mm/min> ; G90
intents.note: wrap in relative/absolute; gate off mid-print

intents.gcode: G28 X | G28 Y | G28 Z
T<n>  (T-1 = deselect; T<n> P0 = skip macros)

print_launch.comment: upload != launch — upload the file (see the paper), then a GCode. Upload-and-hold is the natural default.
print_launch.preferred_oneshot: M32 "0:/gcodes/<file>"  ; select file AND start SD print (one round-trip, minimal race)
two_step: M23 "<path>"  ; select — loads, does NOT start
print_launch.cancel: M0  (M0 H1 to keep heaters) — confirm the safe cancel sequence on hardware
print_launch.confirm_by: no synchronous 'started' ack — poll state.status -> processing AND job.file.fileName == launched path
print_launch.gate: launch only when state.status == idle (RRF refuses a second job while one runs)
print_launch.path_notation: RRF volume notation (0:/gcodes/<file>); read directories.gCodes + enumerate volumes[] rather than hardcode; plain .gcode only (no binary-gcode)
